title: HTTP Semantics, Resources, Methods, and Status Contracts · Execution Trace: Method Safe Idempotent Cacheable
summary: A task service uses POST for every action, mutates state on GET, returns 200 for rejected work, and retries duplicate writes without a resource or method contract. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo method safe idempotent cacheable increment in resource, method, status, and retry semantics table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply safe, idempotent, and cacheable method semantics independently and preserve them through handler behavior.
Separate resource identity, current server state, selected representation, transfer content, and backing storage model.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
